title: OUAS Component Manifest
description: Defines the complete set of UI components an OUAS-compatible application exposes to AI agents. This is the app's menu — agents can only work with what's listed here.

ouas_version.description: The OUAS specification version this manifest conforms to.

app_id.description: Unique reverse-domain identifier for the application.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this manifest was generated.

manifest_version.description: Semantic version of this manifest. Used by the config migrator to detect version mismatches.

components.description: Array of component definitions. Each component describes a UI element the agent can place and configure.

data_sources.description: Array of available data streams that components can bind to.

constraints.description: Global-level constraints the agent must respect.

component_aliases.description: Map of old component IDs to new component IDs. Used by the config migrator to handle component renames across manifest versions.

agent_scope.description: Restricts what AI agents are permitted to modify in this app.

allowed_regions.description: Only Layout Config regions with IDs in this list will be processed. Omit to allow all regions.

locked_components.description: Component IDs in this list cannot be modified by any agent config.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this component. Used in Layout Configs to reference this component.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for developer UIs and documentation.

description.description: Machine-read description for AI agents. Must be precise and unambiguous — describe WHAT the component renders, not how it looks. Minimum 20 characters to enforce quality. (Rule 5.1.2)
examples: A single email item in a list. Shows sender, subject, preview, timestamp.

data_source.description: The data source this component renders. Must match an entry in the manifest's data_sources array. Agent cannot bind a component to a data source not listed here. (Rule 5.1.1)

fields.description: Map of field names to field definitions. These are the data properties the component can display.

slots.description: Named insertion points where other components can be placed within this component.

variants.description: Available visual variants of this component.

constraints.description: Component-level constraints the agent must respect.

locked.description: If true, this component cannot be hidden, moved, or modified by any agent-generated Layout Config.

required.description: If true, this field MUST appear in visible_fields for any Layout Config using this component. (Rule 5.2.3)

values.description: For enum-typed fields, the allowed values.

id.description: Unique identifier for this data source.

description.description: Machine-readable description of what data this source provides.

fields.description: List of available field names in this data source.

min_fields_visible.description: Minimum number of fields that must be in visible_fields.

required_fields_always_visible.description: Fields that must always appear in visible_fields regardless of the config.

max_components_per_layout.description: Maximum number of component regions allowed in a single layout.
